reasoning: The Lean statement quantifies over an arbitrary indexing type `I : Type*` (matching "do not assume the collection is countable"), assumes `[Nonempty I]` (matching "nonempty collection"), takes a family `H : I → Subgroup G` all of whose members are `Normal`, and concludes that the lattice infimum `⨅ i, H i` is `Normal`. In Mathlib, the infimum of a family of subgroups equals the intersection of their underlying sets, so this matches "intersection of the collection of normal subgroups". This is exactly Mathlib's `normal_iInf_normal` lemma. All hypotheses, types, and the conclusion correspond one-to-one with the informal statement.
